titleStmt.title: Letter from Jones McKenna to Lord Wimborne, 21 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Jones McKenna

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Ancient Order of Hibernians Meeting Room, Dingle, Co Kerry, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ancient Order Of Hibernians
Meeting Room
Dingle August 21st 1916 His Excellency
The Right Hon Lord Wimborne
Lord Lieutenant of Ireland
Dublin Castle Your Excellency at a specially convened
meeting of the Dingle branch of the
Ancient. Order. of Hibernians (B O.E) lead at
their Meeting Rooms on Sunday August the 20th
the following resolution was proposed by
Bro Thomas McKenna and seconded by
Bro John Flahive, and directed to be
sent to Your Excellency. With the regular
that you give the matter your
immediate attention as the Fishing Season
is practically at hand. Your most obedient Servant
Jones McKenna (See)
Resolution Resolved that we the members of the
Dingle branch of the Ancient - Order - of - Hibernians
in the meeting assembled, respectfully request
of His Excellency The Right Hon Lord Wimborne
Lord Lieutenant of Ireland to use your His
influence with the Dictricts Board
in from that Body, for the
local Dingle Fishermen a new surrey
of Mackerel nets for the coming Autumn Fishing
as a free grant or a loan on easy terms. We wish to point out to His Excellency. P. J. O.
That it is impossible for the Fishermen
to these mets, as in consequence of
successive bad season (prior to the present
one. The profits of which went towards
defraying the loans and debt incured
during former bad seasons) they are
financially unable to do so. If the [questonable readinig] Board does not
come to the assistance of these poor Fishermen
it will be impossible for them, with their
present worn out nets to profitably work
their Boats during the coming Fall Fishing. And as their principle means of
livelyhood depends on the successful
prosecution of the Mackerel Fishing
we call upon His your Excellency to do all
in His your power to assist these men
and mitigate the great hardships
under which they labour.
